# Ajouter un workflow CI Swift + qualifier le smoke test en GHA

## Contexte

Sur ce repo (`mac-ai-trackers`, app macOS Swift Package), un commit `46c3c02` sur `main` a corrigé une boucle de feedback `NSStatusItem` ↔ KVO `effectiveAppearance` qui pinait le CPU à ~96% (≈600 renders/s du status item). Le fix introduit un `MenuBarRefreshController` couvert par 9 tests unitaires, et un **smoke test d'intégration opt-in** qui :

- s'active via `RUN_SMOKE_TESTS=1`,
- lance le binaire compilé dans une sandbox (`AI_TRACKER_CACHE_DIR`),
- lit un compteur exporté toutes les 2 s par l'app (`AI_TRACKER_EXPORT_RENDER_COUNT`),
- localise le binaire via `AIUT_BINARY_PATH` ou `.build/<triple>/{debug,release}/AIUsagesTrackers`,
- échoue si le compteur dépasse 100 sur 8 s (mutation locale vérifiée : count=4299 quand les verrous sont retirés).

Voir le commit `46c3c02` (squash-merge de la PR #39) pour le détail.

**Problème actuel** : aucun workflow CI ne lance `swift test` sur ce repo. Les workflows présents (`.github/workflows/`) couvrent uniquement la build des PR `assistant-onboarding`, le label gating et les releases.

## Objectif

Faire deux PR séparées.

### PR 1 — Workflow CI général (fondation)

Ajouter `.github/workflows/ci.yml` qui sur chaque `push` et `pull_request` vers `main` :

- tourne sur `macos-15`,
- sélectionne Xcode 26 comme le fait déjà `release.yml` / `assistant-build.yml` (réutiliser le snippet `ls -d /Applications/Xcode_26*.app | sort -V | tail -1`),
- **strippe le plugin SwiftLint du `Package.swift`** comme le fait `assistant-build.yml` (le plugin échoue sur runner GHA avec "prebuild command cannot use executables built from source"),
- lance `swift build` puis `swift test` (la suite courante = 570 tests, durée ~3 s),
- échoue le job si l'un échoue.

Pas de smoke dans cette PR — c'est juste la fondation. Vérifier que le job passe vert avant de merger.

### PR 2 — Qualifier puis intégrer le smoke test

La qualification se fait en **deux PR distinctes**, dans cet ordre :

**PR 2a — Qualification (jetable)** :

1. Crée une branche, ajoute le job `smoke-tests` qui :
   - dépend du job de build,
   - set `RUN_SMOKE_TESTS=1`,
   - lance `swift test --filter StatusItemRenderSmoke`,
   - **est en mode bloquant normal** (pas de `continue-on-error`, on veut le signal franc).
2. Sur la même branche, fais `git revert 46c3c02` pour restaurer l'état pré-fix.
3. Pousse, observe le run CI :
   - **Si le smoke job échoue avec `count >> 100`** → le test discrimine en GHA. Ferme la PR sans merger (elle ne sert qu'à valider).
   - **Si le smoke job passe quand même** → GHA headless ne reproduit pas la boucle (cause connue : `NSStatusItemReplicantShadowView` est instancié par `controlcenter` / `SystemUIServer` côté système, peut ne pas être actif sans utilisateur GUI). Ferme la PR. Le smoke n'a pas sa place en CI cloud — soit l'abandonner, soit le passer en `workflow_dispatch` (déclenchement manuel uniquement).

**PR 2b — Intégration (si la qualification est concluante)** :

Une nouvelle branche, qui contient **uniquement** le job `smoke-tests` (sans le revert). Le job doit passer vert, prouvant que sur main intact le smoke est silencieux. Merge.

## Contraintes du repo à respecter

- Markdown en anglais (voir `.claude/rules/markdown-authoring.md`).
- Pour tout code Swift : charger d'abord les guides `docs/SWIFT-*.md` (concurrency, error handling, etc.) — pas applicable ici puisqu'on touche du YAML, mais vérifier que les éventuelles modifs Swift les respectent.
- Conventional commits comme `ci:` ou `chore(ci):` (voir `docs/GIT-CONVENTIONS.md`).
- Ne pas committer sans demande explicite ; faire d'abord lire le diff par l'utilisateur.

## Livrables attendus

- PR 1 : un seul commit `ci: add Swift build + test workflow on macos-15`, branche `add-ci-workflow`, base `main`.
- PR 2a : branche `qualify-smoke-test-in-gha`, contient `revert "fix(menubar): …"` + ajout du smoke job. Ne pas merger — c'est de la validation, on lit le résultat du run et on ferme.
- PR 2b : ouverte uniquement si PR 2a a démontré que le smoke discrimine. Branche `add-smoke-test-job`, contient uniquement le job. Mergeable.

Démarre par PR 1.

Use when the user asks to harden, make production-ready, handle edge cases, add error states, design empty states, improve onboarding, or fix overflow and i18n issues.
- impeccable:colorize: Add strategic color to features that are too monochromatic or lack visual interest, making interfaces more engaging and expressive. Use when the user mentions the design looking gray, dull, lacking warmth, needing more color, or wanting a more vibrant or expressive palette.
- impeccable:bolder: Amplify safe or boring designs to make them more visually interesting and stimulating. Increases impact while maintaining usability. Use when the user says the design looks bland, generic, too safe, lacks personality, or wants more visual impact and character.
- impeccable:critique: Evaluate design from a UX perspective, assessing visual hierarchy, information architecture, emotional resonance, cognitive load, and overall quality with quantitative scoring, persona-based testing, automated anti-pattern detection, and actionable feedback. Use when the user asks to review, critique, evaluate, or give feedback on a design or component.

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

166	          mkdir -p "${{ github.workspace }}/dist"
167	          ./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh
168	
169	      - name: Smoke-test Info.plist tester-debug keys
170	        env:
171	          EXPECTED_VENDOR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}
172	          EXPECTED_COMMIT: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}
173	          EXPECTED_ISSUE: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.issue_url }}
174	        run: |
175	          set -euo pipefail
176	          PLIST="dist/AI Usages Tracker.app/Contents/Info.plist"
177	          got_vendor=$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :AITrackerVendorDebug' "$PLIST")
178	          got_commit=$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :AITrackerBuildCommit' "$PLIST")

188	        # via the Actions UI when logged in — neither acceptable for testers.
189	        # A GitHub Release exposes the .dmg as a direct, public, unzipped
190	        # asset. We mark it `prerelease: true` so the in-app update checker

192	        # prereleases by design) cannot mistake it for a stable build.

194	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
195	          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}

197	          VENDOR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}
198	          SHORT: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}
199	          SHA: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}

202	          set -euo pipefail

204	          DMG_BASENAME=$(basename "$DMG_FILE")
205	          sha256=$(awk '{print $1}' < "${DMG_FILE}.sha256")
206

208	          # tester sign-offs anyway (they pin the build SHA), so leaving

210	          # Releases page. Same-PR previous builds match the pr<N>- prefix.

212	            --jq ".[] | select(.tagName | startswith(\"assistant-build-pr${PR}-\")) | .tagName")
213	          while IFS= read -r old_tag; do
214	            [ -z "$old_tag" ] && continue
215	            [ "$old_tag" = "$TAG" ] && continue
216	            echo "Deleting stale release ${old_tag}"
217	            gh release delete "$old_tag" --cleanup-tag --yes || true
218	          done <<< "$stale_tags"

220	          notes_lines=(
221	            "Tester DMG built from PR #${PR} at commit \`${SHA}\`."
222	            "Vendor-debug enabled for \`${VENDOR}\`."
223	            ""
224	            "**This release is marked prerelease.** The in-app update checker"
225	            "queries the GitHub \`/releases/latest\` endpoint, which excludes"
226	            "prereleases by design — stable installs will never offer this DMG"
227	            "as an upgrade."
228	          )
229	          notes=$(printf '%s\n' "${notes_lines[@]}")
230	
231	          if gh release view "$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
232	            gh release upload "$TAG" "${DMG_FILE}" "${DMG_FILE}.sha256" --clobber
233	          else
234	            gh release create "$TAG" "${DMG_FILE}" "${DMG_FILE}.sha256" \
235	              --title "Assistant build PR #${PR} · ${VENDOR} · ${SHORT}" \
236	              --notes "${notes}" \
237	              --prerelease \
238	              --target "${SHA}"
239	          fi
240	
241	          download_url="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/${TAG}/${DMG_BASENAME}"
242	          {
243	            echo "sha256=${sha256}"
244	            echo "download_url=${download_url}"
245	            echo "release_tag=${TAG}"
246	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
247	
248	      - name: Post sticky comment on issue
249	        env:
250	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
251	          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
252	          ISSUE: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.issue_number }}
253	          PR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.pr_number }}
254	          VENDOR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}
255	          SHA: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}
256	          SHORT: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}
257	          DMG_NAME: AI-Usages-Tracker-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}.dmg
258	          DMG_SHA: ${{ steps.pointer.outputs.sha256 }}
259	          DOWNLOAD_URL: ${{ steps.pointer.outputs.download_url }}
260	        run: |
261	          set -euo pipefail
262	          SENTINEL='<!-- assistant-build:sticky -->'
263	          # ISO 8601 UTC, minute precision — second precision is noise for a
264	          # human reader and date -u is portable across runners.
265	          BUILT_AT=$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')
266	          body_lines=(
267	            "${SENTINEL}"
268	            "🛠 Build for commit \`${SHORT}\` — vendor-debug enabled for \`${VENDOR}\`"
269	            ""
270	            "Built at: ${BUILT_AT}"
271	            "Full commit: \`${SHA}\`"
272	            "PR: #${PR}"
273	            ""
274	            "- [Download ${DMG_NAME}](${DOWNLOAD_URL}) — SHA-256 \`${DMG_SHA}\`"
275	            "- Alternative: build it yourself from the branch (~5 min) — see [\`docs/CHECKOUT-AND-BUILD.md\`](https://github.com/${REPO}/blob/main/docs/CHECKOUT-AND-BUILD.md)."
276	            ""
277	            "Builds are ad-hoc signed only. First launch: right-click → Open to bypass Gatekeeper."
278	            ""
279	            "### Submitting feedback"
280	            ""
281	            "**Recommended:** open the app, click the \"🧪 Tester feedback\" banner in the popover."
282	            "The form pre-fills the build SHA, vendor, and macOS version, and on submit it"
283	            "opens this issue in your browser with the comment pre-written + reveals the"
284	            "connector log in Finder so you can drag-attach it."
285	            ""
286	            "**Manual fallback:** copy the sign-off template from \`docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md\`,"
287	            "fill it in, attach the log file from \`~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/${VENDOR}-usages-connector.log\`."
288	            ""
289	            "The build sanitizes secrets (tokens, API keys, emails) before writing to the log;"
290	            "if you spot anything that looks confidential, flag it in your comment without"
291	            "quoting the value — it's a bug we want to fix before merge."
292	          )
293	          body=$(printf '%s\n' "${body_lines[@]}")
294	          existing=$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/issues/${ISSUE}/comments" --paginate \
295	            --jq ".[] | select(.body | startswith(\"${SENTINEL}\")) | .id" | head -n1 || true)
296	          if [ -n "$existing" ]; then
297	            gh api -X PATCH "repos/${REPO}/issues/comments/${existing}" -f body="$body" >/dev/null
298	            echo "Updated sticky comment on issue #${ISSUE}"
299	          else
300	            gh issue comment "${ISSUE}" --body "$body"
301	            echo "Posted sticky comment on issue #${ISSUE}"
302	          fi
303	
304	  cleanup-on-close:
305	    # Sweep all prereleases tagged for this PR when it closes (merged or
306	    # not). Without this, every closed PR leaves a dangling tester
307	    # prerelease forever.
308	    if: github.event.action == 'closed'
309	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
310	    permissions:
311	      contents: write
312	    steps:
313	      - name: Delete PR-scoped prereleases and tags
314	        env:
315	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
316	          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
317	          PR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
318	        run: |
319	          set -euo pipefail
320	          tags=$(gh release list --repo "${REPO}" --limit 100 --json tagName \
321	            --jq ".[] | select(.tagName | startswith(\"assistant-build-pr${PR}-\")) | .tagName")
322	          while IFS= read -r tag; do
323	            [ -z "$tag" ] && continue
324	            echo "Deleting release ${tag}"
325	            gh release delete "$tag" --cleanup-tag --yes --repo "${REPO}" || true
326	          done <<< "$tags"

file.content:
name: Assistant change nightly build

# Builds an ad-hoc-signed DMG for any PR linked to a `type:new-assistant`
# or `type:vendor-evolution` issue, with tester-debug mode active for the
# vendor under test. Posts a sticky comment on the linked issue (the
# canonical tester gathering point). See docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md §6.

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, reopened, synchronize, ready_for_review, closed]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      pr_number:
        description: PR number to build
        required: true

concurrency:
  group: assistant-build-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.event.inputs.pr_number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
  issues: write

# The build job overrides `contents` to `write` because it publishes a
# prerelease with the DMG attached. We keep the top-level permission at
# `read` so resolve-issue runs with least privilege.

jobs:
  resolve-issue:
    if: github.event.action != 'closed' && github.event.pull_request.draft != true
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      in_scope: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.in_scope }}
      issue_number: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.issue_number }}
      issue_url: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.issue_url }}
      vendor_slug: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.vendor_slug }}
      pr_number: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.pr_number }}
      head_sha: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.head_sha }}
      head_sha_short: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.head_sha_short }}
      head_ref: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.head_ref }}
    steps:
      - name: Detect linked issue and vendor slug
        id: detect
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.event.inputs.pr_number }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          pr_json=$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}")
          body=$(printf '%s' "$pr_json" | jq -r '.body // ""')
          head_sha=$(printf '%s' "$pr_json" | jq -r '.head.sha')
          head_ref=$(printf '%s' "$pr_json" | jq -r '.head.ref')
          short=${head_sha:0:8}

          # Match `Closes #<n>` (case-insensitive, allow Fixes/Resolves too).
          issue_number=$(printf '%s' "$body" \
            | grep -Eio '(closes|fixes|resolves)[[:space:]]+#[0-9]+' \
            | head -n1 \
            | grep -Eo '[0-9]+' || true)
          if [ -z "$issue_number" ]; then
            echo "No linked issue found in PR body — skipping"
            echo "in_scope=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi

          issue_json=$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/issues/${issue_number}")
          labels=$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | jq -r '[.labels[].name] | join(",")')
          case ",${labels}," in
            *,type:new-assistant,*|*,type:vendor-evolution,*) ;;
            *)
              echo "Issue #${issue_number} not in scope (labels: ${labels}) — skipping"
              echo "in_scope=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              exit 0
              ;;
          esac

          # Vendor slug — pulled from the issue form's machine-readable
          # body. The new-assistant form puts it under the `vendor-slug`
          # input; the vendor-evolution form uses the same id but it's a
          # dropdown, so the value is one of the listed slugs.
          issue_body=$(printf '%s' "$issue_json" | jq -r '.body // ""')
          vendor_slug=$(printf '%s' "$issue_body" \
            | awk '
              /^### Vendor( slug)?/ { found=1; next }
              found && NF { print; exit }
            ' \
            | tr -d '\r' \
            | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' \
            | grep -Eo '^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*' || true)
          if [ -z "$vendor_slug" ]; then
            echo "::warning::Could not parse vendor slug from issue #${issue_number} body; falling back to 'unknown'"
            vendor_slug="unknown"
          fi

          issue_url="https://github.com/${REPO}/issues/${issue_number}"
          {
            echo "in_scope=true"
            echo "issue_number=${issue_number}"
            echo "issue_url=${issue_url}"
            echo "vendor_slug=${vendor_slug}"
            echo "pr_number=${PR_NUMBER}"
            echo "head_sha=${head_sha}"
            echo "head_sha_short=${short}"
            echo "head_ref=${head_ref}"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  build:
    needs: resolve-issue
    if: needs.resolve-issue.outputs.in_scope == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR head
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}

      - name: Select Xcode 26
        # Same prep as release.yml — the codebase requires Swift 6.2.
        run: |
          XCODE_PATH=$(ls -d /Applications/Xcode_26*.app 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1)
          if [ -z "$XCODE_PATH" ]; then
            echo "No Xcode 26.x found on runner. Available:"
            ls -d /Applications/Xcode*.app
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Using: $XCODE_PATH"
          sudo xcode-select -s "$XCODE_PATH/Contents/Developer"

      - name: Show build environment
        run: |
          xcodebuild -version
          swift --version
          echo "macOS: $(sw_vers -productVersion)"
          echo "PR: #${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.pr_number }} (head ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }})"
          echo "Issue: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.issue_url }}"
          echo "Vendor: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}"

      - name: Strip SwiftLint plugin for CI build
        run: |
          # SwiftLintPlugins fails on CI runners with "prebuild command cannot
          # use executables built from source". Lint is a dev/PR concern, not
          # a build-artefact concern.
          python3 -c "
          import re, pathlib
          p = pathlib.Path('AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift')
          s = p.read_text()
          s = re.sub(r',\s*plugins:\s*\[[^\]]*\]', '', s)
          p.write_text(s)
          "

      - name: Build app bundle and DMG
        env:
          VENDOR_DEBUG: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}
          BUILD_COMMIT: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}
          ONBOARDING_ISSUE_URL: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.issue_url }}
          DMG_OUTPUT: ${{ github.workspace }}/dist/AI-Usages-Tracker-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}.dmg
        run: |
          mkdir -p "${{ github.workspace }}/dist"
          ./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh

      - name: Smoke-test Info.plist tester-debug keys
        env:
          EXPECTED_VENDOR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}
          EXPECTED_COMMIT: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}
          EXPECTED_ISSUE: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.issue_url }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          PLIST="dist/AI Usages Tracker.app/Contents/Info.plist"
          got_vendor=$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :AITrackerVendorDebug' "$PLIST")
          got_commit=$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :AITrackerBuildCommit' "$PLIST")
          got_issue=$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :AITrackerOnboardingIssueURL' "$PLIST")
          [ "$got_vendor" = "$EXPECTED_VENDOR" ] || { echo "AITrackerVendorDebug mismatch ($got_vendor vs $EXPECTED_VENDOR)"; exit 1; }
          [ "$got_commit" = "$EXPECTED_COMMIT" ] || { echo "AITrackerBuildCommit mismatch"; exit 1; }
          [ "$got_issue"  = "$EXPECTED_ISSUE" ]  || { echo "AITrackerOnboardingIssueURL mismatch"; exit 1; }
          echo "✓ Info.plist tester-debug keys present and correct"

      - name: Publish DMG as PR prerelease
        id: pointer
        # Workflow artifacts are always zipped by GitHub and only downloadable
        # via the Actions UI when logged in — neither acceptable for testers.
        # A GitHub Release exposes the .dmg as a direct, public, unzipped
        # asset. We mark it `prerelease: true` so the in-app update checker
        # (which queries /releases/latest, an endpoint that excludes
        # prereleases by design) cannot mistake it for a stable build.
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          PR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.pr_number }}
          VENDOR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}
          SHORT: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}
          SHA: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}
          DMG_FILE: dist/AI-Usages-Tracker-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}.dmg
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          TAG="assistant-build-pr${PR}-${VENDOR}-${SHORT}"
          DMG_BASENAME=$(basename "$DMG_FILE")
          sha256=$(awk '{print $1}' < "${DMG_FILE}.sha256")

          # Sweep stale builds for this PR. A new commit invalidates older
          # tester sign-offs anyway (they pin the build SHA), so leaving
          # outdated DMGs around only confuses testers and pollutes the
          # Releases page. Same-PR previous builds match the pr<N>- prefix.
          stale_tags=$(gh release list --limit 100 --json tagName \
            --jq ".[] | select(.tagName | startswith(\"assistant-build-pr${PR}-\")) | .tagName")
          while IFS= read -r old_tag; do
            [ -z "$old_tag" ] && continue
            [ "$old_tag" = "$TAG" ] && continue
            echo "Deleting stale release ${old_tag}"
            gh release delete "$old_tag" --cleanup-tag --yes || true
          done <<< "$stale_tags"

          notes_lines=(
            "Tester DMG built from PR #${PR} at commit \`${SHA}\`."
            "Vendor-debug enabled for \`${VENDOR}\`."
            ""
            "**This release is marked prerelease.** The in-app update checker"
            "queries the GitHub \`/releases/latest\` endpoint, which excludes"
            "prereleases by design — stable installs will never offer this DMG"
            "as an upgrade."
          )
          notes=$(printf '%s\n' "${notes_lines[@]}")

          if gh release view "$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            gh release upload "$TAG" "${DMG_FILE}" "${DMG_FILE}.sha256" --clobber
          else
            gh release create "$TAG" "${DMG_FILE}" "${DMG_FILE}.sha256" \
              --title "Assistant build PR #${PR} · ${VENDOR} · ${SHORT}" \
              --notes "${notes}" \
              --prerelease \
              --target "${SHA}"
          fi

          download_url="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/${TAG}/${DMG_BASENAME}"
          {
            echo "sha256=${sha256}"
            echo "download_url=${download_url}"
            echo "release_tag=${TAG}"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Post sticky comment on issue
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          ISSUE: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.issue_number }}
          PR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.pr_number }}
          VENDOR: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}
          SHA: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha }}
          SHORT: ${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}
          DMG_NAME: AI-Usages-Tracker-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.vendor_slug }}-${{ needs.resolve-issue.outputs.head_sha_short }}.dmg
          DMG_SHA: ${{ steps.pointer.outputs.sha256 }}
          DOWNLOAD_URL: ${{ steps.pointer.outputs.download_url }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          SENTINEL='<!-- assistant-build:sticky -->'
          # ISO 8601 UTC, minute precision — second precision is noise for a
          # human reader and date -u is portable across runners.
          BUILT_AT=$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')
          body_lines=(
            "${SENTINEL}"
            "🛠 Build for commit \`${SHORT}\` — vendor-debug enabled for \`${VENDOR}\`"
            ""
            "Built at: ${BUILT_AT}"
            "Full commit: \`${SHA}\`"
            "PR: #${PR}"
            ""
            "- [Download ${DMG_NAME}](${DOWNLOAD_URL}) — SHA-256 \`${DMG_SHA}\`"
            "- Alternative: build it yourself from the branch (~5 min) — see [\`docs/CHECKOUT-AND-BUILD.md\`](https://github.com/${REPO}/blob/main/docs/CHECKOUT-AND-BUILD.md)."
            ""
            "Builds are ad-hoc signed only. First launch: right-click → Open to bypass Gatekeeper."
            ""
            "### Submitting feedback"
            ""
            "**Recommended:** open the app, click the \"🧪 Tester feedback\" banner in the popover."
            "The form pre-fills the build SHA, vendor, and macOS version, and on submit it"
            "opens this issue in your browser with the comment pre-written + reveals the"
            "connector log in Finder so you can drag-attach it."
            ""
            "**Manual fallback:** copy the sign-off template from \`docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md\`,"
            "fill it in, attach the log file from \`~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/${VENDOR}-usages-connector.log\`."
            ""
            "The build sanitizes secrets (tokens, API keys, emails) before writing to the log;"
            "if you spot anything that looks confidential, flag it in your comment without"
            "quoting the value — it's a bug we want to fix before merge."
          )
          body=$(printf '%s\n' "${body_lines[@]}")
          existing=$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/issues/${ISSUE}/comments" --paginate \
            --jq ".[] | select(.body | startswith(\"${SENTINEL}\")) | .id" | head -n1 || true)
          if [ -n "$existing" ]; then
            gh api -X PATCH "repos/${REPO}/issues/comments/${existing}" -f body="$body" >/dev/null
            echo "Updated sticky comment on issue #${ISSUE}"
          else
            gh issue comment "${ISSUE}" --body "$body"
            echo "Posted sticky comment on issue #${ISSUE}"
          fi

  cleanup-on-close:
    # Sweep all prereleases tagged for this PR when it closes (merged or
    # not). Without this, every closed PR leaves a dangling tester
    # prerelease forever.
    if: github.event.action == 'closed'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - name: Delete PR-scoped prereleases and tags
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          PR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          tags=$(gh release list --repo "${REPO}" --limit 100 --json tagName \
            --jq ".[] | select(.tagName | startswith(\"assistant-build-pr${PR}-\")) | .tagName")
          while IFS= read -r tag; do
            [ -z "$tag" ] && continue
            echo "Deleting release ${tag}"
            gh release delete "$tag" --cleanup-tag --yes --repo "${REPO}" || true
          done <<< "$tags"

name: CI

# Runs `swift build` and `swift test` on every push and pull request
# targeting `main`. This is the baseline correctness gate for the Swift
# package; per-vendor smoke checks live in separate workflows.

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Select Xcode 26
        # Same prep as release.yml / assistant-build.yml — the codebase
        # requires Swift 6.2.
        run: |
          XCODE_PATH=$(ls -d /Applications/Xcode_26*.app 2>/dev/null | sort -V | tail -1)
          if [ -z "$XCODE_PATH" ]; then
            echo "No Xcode 26.x found on runner. Available:"
            ls -d /Applications/Xcode*.app
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Using: $XCODE_PATH"
          sudo xcode-select -s "$XCODE_PATH/Contents/Developer"

      - name: Show build environment
        run: |
          xcodebuild -version
          swift --version
          echo "macOS: $(sw_vers -productVersion)"

      - name: Strip SwiftLint plugin for CI build
        run: |
          # SwiftLintPlugins fails on CI runners with "prebuild command
          # cannot use executables built from source". Lint is a dev/PR
          # concern, enforced locally and in Xcode — not a CI gate.
          python3 -c "
          import re, pathlib
          p = pathlib.Path('AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift')
          s = p.read_text()
          s = re.sub(r',\s*plugins:\s*\[[^\]]*\]', '', s)
          p.write_text(s)
          "

      - name: Build
        working-directory: AIUsagesTrackers
        run: swift build

      - name: Test
        working-directory: AIUsagesTrackers
        run: swift test
